Under the Post Components section, if the HU is posted using the Post HU button, the system will perform a goods issue for those products in the outbound delivery, indicating that the products have been consumed for the kitting process.

Now Let’s pack them in new HU for processing

The system navigates to the packing work center, where the final product is packed into a new HU, as shown below. After packing, save the changes, and upon returning to the previous screen, the newly packed HU will be listed in the kitting work center bin.

When the newly packed HU containing the final product is posted by clicking the Post HU button, a Goods Receipt is recorded, indicating that the final product has been successfully created in the system.

Next, click on “Complete Process Step for HU” and save the changes. The system will automatically create a warehouse task (52273) to move the product from the work center to the designated storage bin, initiating the put away process.

A warehouse task is created to move the product from KTS_BIN1 to the E101-04-03 bin. This bin serves as the final storage location for the kit header product. Upon confirmation, the put away process will also be completed in the inbound delivery.

Let’s check the bin for final product

Finally, the kit product is available for sale with stock type F2. When an order for this product is received, it can be directly picked from the designated bin and shipped.

entry.title: Automatic Generation of Packaging item for Goods issue S4 HANA
entry.content:
Introduction:-

This blog includes configuration and master data set up to achieve automatic generation of packing items in delivery document.

Business requirement :-

When business need to have visibility of what packaging material used in deliveries as well as how many quantity of packing material has been consumed to prepare shipment. then we have to perform below stated configurations and master data setup to achieve business requirement.

 

To achieve requirement below configuration has to be set up.

Configuration

1. Define Packaging material types

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Packaging Material Types for Shipping.

2. Define packaging material group for packing material.

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Material Group for Packaging Materials

3. Define Allowed packing material types.

SPRO--> Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Packing-->Define Allowed Packaging Materials

4. Define packaging material at EWM

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->Basics-->Define Packaging Material Types for Warehousing

5. Maintain Allowed Packaging Material Types for Packing Group

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->Basics-->Maintain Allowed Packaging Material Types for Packing Group

6. Assign number ranges for Packaging material

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Cross-Process Settings-->Handling Units-->External Identification-->Assign Number Range Intervals to Packaging Material Types

7. Activate automatic generation of packaging items.

SPRO--> SCM Extended Warehouse Management-->Extended Warehouse Management-->Goods Issue Process-->Outbound Delivery-->Automatically Generate Packaging Items.

Activate Delivery item for combination of warehouse and Delivery type.

select warehouse line and double click on Relevance of Pack materials.

Select line and click on Determination of Delivery Item Type and maintain delivery type item type and stock type

in this configuration assign packing material to EWM document type and item type OPHU and select stock type as F2.

8. For ECC delivery type activate automatic packaging indicator.

SPRO-->Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Deliveries-->Define Delivery Types.

9. Assign packaging material  item categories to delivery type with usage of PACK.

SPRO-->Logistics Execution-->Shipping-->Deliveries-->Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries.

Master Data set up

1. Create Packaging material and assign packaging material type and group in sales general/plant data view what we have created in above configuration

2. If Packaging material is inventory managed then goods movement bin should be maintained in transaction

/SCWM/GMBIN_DET.

Test case:-

  • Create sales order by using transaction Tcode VA01.
  • Create delivery with reference to sales order using Tcode VL01N.
  • Perform Picking activity in EWM
  • During Packing select packing material EP1RA-01 to perform packing in transaction /SCWM/PACK for EWM delivery.
  • Performing Goods issue from EWM deliver.

After Goods issue ODO contains packing material along with quantity

in ECC delivery also packaging material will appear

 

 

Conclusion:-

By Performing all above configuration and master data set up packaging material will start populating in EWM delivery as well as in ECC delivery with quantity which has been consumed perform packing in work-center.

entry.title: SAP EWM- Automatic Assortment Packing of HU in outbound delivery using UPB
entry.content:
This blog is intended to showcase how outbound delivery can be packed with an Assortment Packing (Mix HU) using the UPB (Unified Package Building).

Assortment Packing is packing of multiple different products into one package and mainly observed in Fashion industry. Also, in other industries such as Packaged sweets, Nuts & Candies, Cosmetics, Garment, Paint industry etc.

Business Scenario: User will pick material from bins of different storage types. After picking user will move products to Work center for packing. User expects system should pack products and create Ship HUs automatically. HU labels will be printed and pasted for shipping further.

  • Configuration

1) Creation of Packaging Specification

SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Condition TablesSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Access SequencesSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Condition TypesSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Define Determination ProcedureSCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Packaging Specification > Determination of Packaging Specifications > Create Condition Maintenance Group

2) Creation of UPB Profile (Unified Package Building)

SCM EWM > EWM > Cross-Process Settings > Handling Units > Unified Package Building > Define Unified Package Building Profile

3) Assign UPB to Document Type

SCM EWM > EWM > Goods Issue Process > Outbound Delivery > Define Document Types for Outbound Delivery Process 

For Outbound delivery Doc type OUTB and doc type PDO. 

4) Work Center Configuration

SCM EWM > EWM > Master Data > Work Center >Specify Work Center Layout

For a Warehouse Work center layout (Eg. VPMX), Pack Automatically should be active.

  • Master Data 

1) Packaging Specification

Click on Create button and Select correct Packaging Specification group. Also Maintain condition records with a correct condition type.

If we are using reference packaging material, checkbox for Ref. Mix Allowed should be ticked. If there are batches which can be mixed checkbox Batch Mix Allowed should be ticked.

2) Reference Packaging material in Material Master

In S4 Material master in Basic Data 1 view or Sales: General/ Plant view maintain reference packaging material in which the product should be packed. It will be updated same in EWM product master.

  • Testing
  1.  Create Sale Order (VA01) 
  2. Create Outbound Delivery (VL01N)
  3. Open Outbound Delivery in EWM (/N/SCWM/PRDO)

  4. Create Warehouse Task/ Warehouse Order

  5. Open and Confirm Warehouse Order
    All the products are picked in a single Pick HU. Once all the warehouse tasks confirmed, WO will be confirmed.

  6. Go to Pack Workcenter (/N/SCWM/PACK)

    In Control Tab provide default bin. If we use POSC, then no need to enter bin manually.

    Select HU and click on pack button. Make sure Picking status should be completed. Based on Packaging specification Products are packed into different HUs. We can print the Handling Units labels and can be pasted on new Handling Units. 

  7. Once all the products are packed outbound delivery status changed to Completed.

entry.title: EWM Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) – A simple guide
entry.content:
This post will show you how to set up and test Layout-Oriented Storage Control (LOSC) in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM). We'll focus on the customization and steps to follow the LOSC process. To start, let's imagine this scenario in an imaginary warehouse:



Step 1: Arrival at the Warehouse (Storage Type 9010)
Goods arrive at the warehouse dock and are unloaded into storage type 9010. They are scanned and entered the EWM system, starting their journey.

Step 2: Quality Inspection (Storage Type 0030)
Next, the goods move to storage type 0030 for quality inspection. Here, each item is checked to ensure it meets the required standards.

Step 3: Intermediate Storage (Storage Type 0035)
After passing the quality check, the goods are transferred to storage type 0035, an intermediate storage area. This is a temporary holding zone.

Step 4: Final Storage (Storage Type 0040)
Finally, the goods reach storage type 0040, the final storage area. Here, products are organized and stored until they are ready to be picked up and shipped to customers.


CUSTOMIZING STEPS

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Master Data -> Define Storage Types

Note: ensure that you have created the activity areas and storage bins for the storage types used in this process. Also, execute the bins sorting.

 

 

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Warehouse Process Type

Note: The WTP for "LOSC" was copied from 1010. Ensure the "Select HU w/o Storage Process" field is checked, as LOSC only works for handling unit warehouse tasks. If unchecked, a product warehouse task will be created, and the process won't work as intended.

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Control Indicators for Determining Warehouse Process Types

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Determine Warehouse Process Type

 

  • SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Task -> Define Layout-Oriented Storage Process Control

 Note: In this scenario, we're using the same WPT for LOSC movements. For better traceability, you can create a WPT copied from 3030 to separate the LOSC steps.


TESTING
 

  • Step 1 – Goods receipt

    T-Code: MIGO

Inboud delivery created:

 

  • Step 2 – Inbound Delivery

T-Code: /SCWM/PRDI
Note: WT we’re created automatically using a PPF action but this is a subject to another post.


Note: HU we’re automatically created using packing specification.

 

  • Step 3 – Warehouse Management Monitor

T-Code: /SCWM/MON
Two warehouse tasks (WT) were created because we are using LOSC. The first warehouse task remains with status "B – Waiting":


The second warehouse task uses WPT "3030" to move the handling unit from 9010 to 0030 and has the status "Open":


Confirm the second WT:

A third warehouse task is created automatically when the second is confirmed:


The first WT has the source storage type and bin updated but remains with status "B – Waiting":


The third warehouse task is created to move the HU from 0030 to 0035:

Confirm the third WT:


The first WT has been updated and then the last move of HU is done:


Confirm the first WT:

Process done!


CONCLUSION

Using SAP EWM LOSC helps the warehouse efficiently manage the flow of goods through different storage types. This process ensures products are inspected, stored, and organized in the best way possible, leading to faster order fulfillment and higher customer satisfaction.

I try to make it simple, hope this guide helps you guys.

Best Regards,
Yuri Nogueira
SAP EWM Consultant

entry.title: Assessment with Logistics questions to support the explore phase (covering SAP EWM, TM, YL and BN4L)
entry.content: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1RsU-b60k0bvqpTD3oK2QO0DtLFzbfdNHwmlpswG9Phk/edit?gid=0#gid=0

entry.title: Process to Handle Damage Goods While Performing Putaway
entry.content:
Damage goods should be properly managed to avoid disruptions and preserve the integrity of the warehouse stock. If goods are found to be damaged after the initial scanning or inspection, warehouse operators can manually update the system to move them to the damaged goods area, ensuring they do not interfere with usable inventory.

Let us see the simple configuration required for doing Putaway process 

  1. Create Separate Stock Type <Z6- Blocked in Warehouse> 
  2. Configure Stock type --> here we assign the Z6 to availability Group which is mapped to Sloc via Node  -->Map Storage Locations from ERP System to EWM

2.Create Separate Storage Type < May be not re-quired but to keep the segregation Clean and efficient can create based on Business decision>

  1. Create Separate Bin under any Storage Type where you want Putaway.

Process Flow:

 Steps:

 

 

  1. Create Purchase Order 
  2. Create Inbound Delivery
  3. Open RFUI ---> Go to Inbound Process

  1. Perform Receiving of  Handling Units 

  1. Receive HU by Delivery ---> Put the Inbound Delivery

  1. Choose the Handling Unit --> Put  or Scan the HU Number

  1. Post Goods Receipt of the Handling Unit

  1. Now Perform Putaway ---> Choose the Exception Code SPPB to Change the Actual Quantity of Putaway

  1. Next Choose the Exception Code CHBD to change the Destination Bin for the Damaged Product 

  1. Atlast check the Stock Situation in the Warehouse 

Benefits :

  • Identifying and marking damaged goods at the time of Putaway ensures that warehouse workers can take corrective actions immediately, such as returning the damaged goods to the supplier, disposing of them, or storing them in a designated damaged goods area.
  • This reduces the risk of unnecessary stock moves or errors during future picking processes, as damaged goods are properly segregated and managed.

entry.title: Inventory Management: Inventory Counting Process - Cycle Counting (IM-MM)
entry.content:
What is inventory counting?

Basically, this is a process of verifying and recording the actual stock levels in a storage location or warehouse to ensure they match the data in the system. In physical counting, we need to shut down the entire operation and count everything. Until everything’s counted, we cannot resume executing any operation like picking, packing, and shipping.

Counting Physical Inventory Flow

 

Now let’s talk about cycle counting. 

Cycle counting is a method of counting inventory periodically based on predefined criteria, such as the frequency set by ABC classification. This counting is where a subset of inventory items is counted on a rotating schedule rather than conducting a full physical inventory at once. It helps in maintaining inventory accuracy while minimizing operational disruptions.

ABC cycle counting uses the Pareto Principal method to prioritize inventory into several tiers by the level of importance. Items that generate the most revenue are counted most frequently.

For example, a fast moving and high value material will be counted several times a year while slow moving and low value material can be counted once a year. Here is the example calculation:

Example calculation of ABC cycle counting

SAP will calculate the value for each material in the plant based on the consumption value, requirement value, and movement type. Here are the steps to see the ABC Cycle Counting analysis:

ABC cycle counting in SAP

 

  • Based on movement type:
    1. Configuration: (T-Code: OMCO)

    Configuration

    Number of cycle counts: Number of times / year that you want to count the material.
    PCT (%) Percentage: % of materials that you assign to ABC classification, ranking from high to low.

    2. Set Indicator: MM01 (Plant data/Stor.1 Tab)

    You can set the cycle counting indicator manually from the material master.

    Cycle counting in material master

    Physical inventory indicator for cycle counting indicates that the given material is subject to the cycle counting method of inventory. The indicator also defines at which time intervals a physical inventory is to be carried out for the material.


    CC indicator is fixed, specifies that the cycle counting indicator cannot be changed for cycle counting by an ABC analysis. The only way to change this indicator is through manual maintenance of the material master record. Set this indicator if the material is always to remain in the specified cycle counting category.

    3. ABC Analysis: (T-Code: MIBC)

    Analysis based on consumption value

    Consumptions in MIBC are based on movement types and on configuration settings for movement types.


    Summary of counting effort per fiscal year

    If you click the Display Counting Effort, you will see the summary of how many materials in every category and how many times they need to be counted in one plant. This will help businesses to decide the inventory counting schedule without taking time to do manual analysis.

  • Based on Consumption
    1.     Configuration: SPRO → Logistic → Material Master → Settings for Key Fields → Extend ABC Indicator

    ABC indicator for materials

    We can set the indicator based on what the business needs. This indicator classifies a material as an A, B, or C part according to its consumption value.

    2. Set Indicator: (T-Code: MM01 (MRP 1))

    ABC indicator in material master MRP 1

    3. ABC Analysis: (T-Code: MC40)

    Analysis strategy

    This report provides ABC Analysis based on the consumption / usage of material.

    Analysis based on the consumption / usage value

    You can also see the double-line analysis that shows the quantity. This will help businesses to decide which material needs to be counted more frequently based on the consumption.

  • Based on requirement value
    The configuration and where to set the indicator are the same. But for the analysis result, you can check it on MC41.

    ABC Analysis: (T-Code: MC41)

    Analysis strategy

    This report provides ABC Analysis based on the requirement (MRP) of material.

    Analysis based on the requirement (MRP) of material

    Then, how to enter and adjust inventory counting?

    Firstly, we need to know there are several stock types in an inventory. Each type will have a different movement type based on the stock's conditions. If there is any difference with the book stocks after counting the physical stocks, the book stocks need to be adjusted to the physical stocks. 

    Movement types

    Now we’re going to talk about how to do the inventory counting in SAP.

    Here are the steps to enter the inventory counting:

    Inventory counting process flow


    1. Go to MI01 to create an inventory document.

    Create PI document

    Posting Block = Indicates that no goods movements involving the materials listed in the physical inventory document can be posted for the duration of a physical inventory count.

    Stock Type = Shows the stock type for which a physical inventory is to be carried out (Warehouse, Quality Inspection, Warehouse/Quality Inspection, Blocked).

    2. Perform counting and enter the inventory counting result in MI04.
    Insert the physical inventory document number that has been created before.

    Enter inventory count

    Insert the physical inventory quantity that has been counted. Specifies the quantity to be moved in the unit of entry. The quantity is automatically converted to the stockkeeping unit (base unit of measure).

    Enter quantity

    3. Checklist of Inventory Differences in MI20.
    The difference quantity between the physical inventory and system stock will be shown in this window.

    List of difference

    The aim of posting differences is to reconcile stocks in the ERP system and in particular, in financial accounting.
    List of inventory differences

    4. Post inventory differences in MI07

    Post inventory differences: Initial screen

    Reason for inventory difference = Choose the reason based on the movement type. The movement type can be defined by the stock's condition.

    Post inventory differences

    5. Check material document in MMBE (Not mandatory)

    In the stock overview, you can select the stocks that have been entered based on the physical inventory quantity and see the material document. There you will see that the materials are reduced by 5 pcs. Using the right movement type will automatically execute the adjustment stocks between book stocks and physical stocks.

    Material document list

    6. Post Document Count and Differences in MI10

    Post document

    7. Print preview in MI21 (Not mandatory)

    Print preview


    So that's basically how you do the cycle counting in SAP. By implementing cycle counting, organizations can systematically verify inventory levels without requiring a full physical count, reducing operational disruptions while improving data accuracy. SAP cycle counting process supports continuous inventory accuracy, leading to better decision-making and optimized operational performance.

    Feel free to discuss more if you have any other insights or questions!



    Cheers,
    Dafina

entry.title: Batch, Serial, and Quality Management in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Managing electronic components demands precision, accuracy, and stringent quality control. Without an efficient system, tracking these products can become overwhelming, leading to risks such as counterfeits, defective parts, and inventory discrepancies. SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) simplifies this process through Serial Number Management, Batch Management, and Quality Control, ensuring streamlined and efficient warehouse operations.

Serial Number Management in SAP EWM ensures precise tracking by assigning unique serial numbers to items, enabling real-time traceability, warranty tracking, and counterfeit prevention at the inventory level. Batch Management organizes stock based on production batches, utilizing FIFO and FEFO strategies to optimize inventory handling, prevent obsolescence, and improve order accuracy. Quality Management safeguards product integrity by triggering inspections upon goods receipt, blocking defective stock, and enabling returns or rework to ensure only high-quality products enter production or reach customers.

Let’s dive into the configuration to achieve this requirement!

Configuration:

1. Specify Batch Level and Activate Status Management

2. Maintain Internal Batch Number Assignment Range

3. Define LE Serial Number Profile

4. Define Serial Number Profile (Other SAP Components)

5. Define Warehouse Number Dependent Serial Number Profiles

6.Serial Number Settings for Warehouse Number

7. Quality Management

  I. Generate Inspection object types versions

 II. Maintain Inspection object type

III. Maintain Follow Up Actions

IV. Assign Logistical Follow up Actions

 V. S4- Maintain Inspection Types

VI. S4 – Maintain Settings at Plant Level

VII. S4- Define Follow-up Actions for Usage Decision

VIII. S4-Edit Code Groups and code for usage Decisions

 IX. S4- Edit Selected sets for usage decisions

8. Warehouse Process Type

1. Define Warehouse Process Type

2. Define process type determination indicator

3. Determine Warehouse Process Type

9.Putaway Strategies

1. Define STSS for Put away

2. Assign Storage Types to STSS

3. Specify Storage type search sequence for put away

10. Create Material (Serial, Batch & Quality Managed)

The screenshots provided pertain exclusively to the serial, batch, and quality management parameters that must be maintained in the material.

Extend this material to Product master data in SAP EWM and extend storage location as well.

Test Case

1. Create Purchase Order- ME21N

2. Create Inbound Delivery & distribute to EWM- VL31N

With reference to the PO, an Inbound Delivery is created, including Batch and Serial Numbers. As a prerequisite, the batch must first be created using the transaction code MSC1N, as shown in the screenshot below.

 

If the vendor sends an Advance Shipping Notice (ASN) containing batch and serial number information, the system automatically creates a batch and assigns the corresponding serial numbers to the respective inbound delivery. In this scenario, manually created a batch and entered the details before assigning the serial number to the respective delivery. This ensures proper tracking and alignment of batch and serial information within the system.

3. Maintain Inbound delivery in EWM- /N/SCWM/PRDI

The delivery is distributed to EWM along with the batch and serial numbers. In SAP EWM, batches and serial numbers can also be created independently. If a batch is created in EWM, it is automatically replicated in S/4HANA to maintain consistency. Similarly, serial numbers generated in EWM are also distributed to S/4HANA, ensuring synchronization between both systems for seamless inventory tracking and management.

As soon as the goods receipt is posted in SAP EWM, the system automatically generates an inspection document, as shown in the image below. This inspection document is created because the product is quality-managed, ensuring that it undergoes the necessary quality checks before being released into inventory.

4. Inspection Lot- QA32

Since this product is batch-managed, you can search for the inspection lot using the batch number when making a usage decision. Once usage decision saved in inspection lot, An warehouse task will be created in EWM for put away.

5. Warehouse Monitor- /N/SCWM/MON

The warehouse tasks are generated based on the follow-up action configured in Step 7 (IV). The assigned Warehouse Process Type within the follow-up action triggers the creation of these tasks. Once these tasks are confirmed, the products are efficiently moved to their designated storage bins, ensuring seamless warehouse operations and accurate inventory placement. If the products are rejected, they will be moved to a scrap storage type. However, if they are returnable, they will be transferred to a quarantine storage area for temporary holding before being returned at a later stage.

In conclusion, managing electronic components efficiently requires a system that ensures tracking, quality control, and accurate inventory management. SAP EWM helps achieve this through Serial Number Management, Batch Management, and Quality Control, making warehouse operations smoother and more reliable. By using these features, businesses can reduce risks, prevent counterfeits, minimize waste, and improve efficiency. This ensures that only high-quality products make it to customers, strengthening the overall supply chain.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Extended Warehouse Management Private Edition 2023 FPS03 – What’s New
entry.content:
We have recently released the latest updates for SAP EWM with the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 3 update. The enhancements of this release further improve our Advanced Shipping & Receiving integration and functionalities, complementing the features we delivered in the SAP S/4HANA 2023 Feature Pack 2 release. Furthermore, with this release we introduced a new Machine Learning based feature, called “Predictive Labor Demand Planning”, empowering shift managers at the warehouse to more accurately and efficiently plan out the tasks for the day. And to start today’s deep dive, we will begin with it.

PREDICITVE LABOR DEMAND PLANNING

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release, we deliver the new Machine-Learning based predictive labor demand planning (LDP) that can be used to predict planned workload data based on the picking and packing outbound processes. This enables warehouse shift managers and supervisors to plan resources and workloads more efficiently. Very summarily, LDP uses historical workload records to calculate a planned duration for warehouse workload records, and then use those records to plan outbound delivery orders.

 Historical workload records contain information about executed warehouse tasks (WTs) in the warehouse. They are not stored in an archive as all data is saved in the database, and outdated records are deleted. These records are only written for picking and packing tasks when a warehouse order is confirmed. These records contain application information that is mainly based on warehouse task data, with additional data from the assigned outbound delivery order.

The planned duration is a key component of a planned workload record. In labor management, you can use engineered labor standards to calculate the planned duration, but in labor demand planning, this is not necessary. Instead, labor demand planning uses historical workload records to calculate the planned duration for planned workload records.

Please note that if you have already set up engineered labor standards, they will still be used to calculate the planned duration. In this case, the system will not calculate based on historical workload records. You can also use engineered labor standards for one area of your warehouse and use calculations based on historical workload records for another area.

Outbound delivery order (ODO) processing includes picking and packing steps before the ODO item can be staged and loaded. You can use labor demand planning to get better visibility into the planned workload that is expected from outbound delivery orders. This allows you to predict peaks in the workload and balance the workload of the warehouse accordingly. The way LDP calculates ODO planned workloads is the following:

  1. The system searches for historical data that belongs to similar ODO items. The resulting data forms the basis of the calculations for the required data of the current ODO item.
  2. The system identifies the picking location, preferably by using rough bin determination.
  3. The system determines the external process step that is most used by similar ODO items.
  4. If similar ODO items require packing steps, the system calculates the packing steps based on the packing-picking ratio of the similar ODO items. The packing-picking ratio indicates how often the pick step of an outbound delivery order with similar attributes was followed by a pack step and is calculated from the historical workload data. Single pieces may need a pack step while whole pallet picks don’t need to be packed in addition.

 With LDP, it is possible to view planned workload durations directly in the warehouse monitor, allowing for better visibility and control over task planning. This functionality improves operational efficiency by streamlining the planning process and ensuring more accurate and timely execution of warehouse tasks as well as reduce inefficiencies by predicting the time required for tasks such as picking, packing, and outbound deliveries.

This is only a first step for Predictive Labor Demand Planning, and we plan to build on top of this feature with future releases. But we will reveal those steps at a future date.

ADVANCED SHIPPING & RECEIVING UPDATES

With the SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 release we continue to further extend the range of functionalities available with Advanced Shipping & Receiving as well as expand on existing ones. In the interest of time and convenience, I want to briefly touch upon every ASR update in one or two paragraphs, and we begin with integration to Global Trade Service:

  • Integration of Decentralized EWM and TM with GTS – This is a new integration of decentralized Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) and Transportation Management (TM) with SAP Global Trade Services (GTS) which supports the execution of the outbound, or export, process. It enables users to conduct compliance checks for freight units and freight orders and complete export declaration for consignments and freight orders.

  • Search for Advanced Shipping and Receiving Deliveries in Waves – In this release, we further build upon a feature we delivered in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS02, by enabling users to search for waves, wave items, and wave-relevant warehouse requests that are part of Advanced Shipping and Receiving deliveries in the Maintain Waves app (transaction /SCWM/WAVE) and the warehouse monitor (transaction /SCWM/MON).
  • Custom Checks for Loading Materials onto a Freight Order – We also provide BAdIs that add customer-specific checks for handling units or warehouse requests before loading execution in Advanced Shipping and Receiving. The checks take place during outbound delivery, and allow for the customer’s own logic for loading checks in:
    • The radio frequency (RF) screen before loading, where the system can show special warning or error message to the RF user
    • The App "Load or Unload Freight Orders", where the same or a similar BAdI is called when the loading execution function is used
    • The enterprise warehouse management (EWM) desktop transaction /SCWM/LOAD
  • TM Updates for Unpacked Products with Actual EWM Weight and Volume – With this update, when information regarding weight and volume for unpacked products in an outbound delivery order item is updated in Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), the relevant documents in Transportation Management (TM) are updated with the adjusted EWM weight and volume information. These updates are made after the “Ready for Shipping” status is set in EWM. The updated weight and volume are sent on product item level and on handling unit header level.

CONTINUOUS IMPROVEMENTS

To round of today’s overview, I would like to also go over some of the final topics that came to be as a result of the most recent Customer Connect program:

  • Add Short Text to Goods Issue and Cancellation - With this feature, it is now possible to provide a short text for goods issues and cancellations in several apps. A text of up to 50 characters can now be manually entered, for example, to add an explanation or other information to the goods movement in the following apps: “Manage Physical Stock – Products” (F5340); “Manage Warehouse Documents” (F5341). This text can be seen in the relevant material documents in other components of SAP S/4HANA using the “Display Material Document List” app. If synchronous goods movements are used from Inventory Management to Extended Warehouse Management (only available in EWM embedded in SAP S/4HANA), text can be entered in the “Post Goods Movement” app (transaction MIGO). The text is visible in the corresponding goods movement document in EWM, and can be seen in the monitor node at Documents -> Warehouse Task. This functionality has also been added to the consume and scrap actions in the “Warehouse Physical Stock by Product” API.

  • Scanning Alternative HU IDs During RF Processing - With this feature, it is now possible to scan alternative handling unit (HU) IDs on vendor labels which the system converts them to internal Extended Warehouse Management Handling Unit IDs. Converting the alternative HU IDs in RF transactions removes the need to re-label the HUs or create custom RF screens. Note that if multiple HU IDs are assigned to one alternative HU ID, the relevant internal HU ID can be selected.

FIND OUT MORE!

To see more details on the above mentioned topics as well as see what other enhancements we delivered with this release, I highly encourage you to explore our latest “SAP EWM in SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS03 – What’s New” slide deck.

You can also find more technical details and documentation on the above listed topic and others we left out via the “What’s New” viewer on the Help Documentation portal.

For more information about SAP Extended Warehouse Management, please follow us on social media, our YouTube channel or our community pages:

SAP EWM Community

SAP YouTube Channel

EWM LinkedIn Community

SAP Extended Warehouse Management Roadmap Explorer

Release information and restrictions of embedded EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

Release information and restrictions of Decentralized EWM on SAP S/4HANA 2023

For a complete list of Q&A from the EWM community, please access this link:
https://answers.sap.com/tags/01200615320800000705

In case you do not find your specific question there, feel free to post your question via the following form:
https://answers.sap.com/questions/ask.html?primaryTagId=01200615320800000705

entry.title: Guide to Mass Uploading Product Master in EWM using Warehouse Monitor & /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT
entry.content:
In this document, I will explain how to mass upload the product master in the SAP EWM system within S4HANA using the warehouse monitor and /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT.

When you create a material using the MM01 transaction, your master data will automatically transfer to the EWM system due to the embedded connection.

For example, if you create the material MAT200 in MM01, you can see the material data created in ERP tables such as MARA, MARD, and MARC.However, if you attempt to view the same data in the T code /SCWM/MAT1(warehouse level), you will encounter an error. This occurs because the material created in MM01 means that the global data of the material has been established, making it accessible for all SAP modules such as EWM, TM, MDG, etc. This is a functionality of the embedded system.In the transaction code /SAPAPO/MAT1, you can view all basic global-level data created in the ERP system.Now, we will focus on extending product data with the warehouse view in the EWM system.

To accomplish this, we will use the transaction code /SCWM/MAT1, which allows us to extend both the warehouse view of the product and specific storage types.

It's important to note the following:

- Your warehouse view data will be stored in the database table /SAPAPO/MATLWH ( not a product as a direct entry, pass MATNR to /SAPAPO/MATKEY and get the MATID then pass here).

- Data related to warehouse storage types will be stored in the database table /SAPAPO/MATLWHST.

Here is an example of this,

I have maintained the warehouse data but not the storage-type data. Now let's see how it appears on the tables.Mass upload product master in S4 HANA EWM:

Mass uploading of the product master in EWM means extending the warehouse view/storage type view for materials that have already been created in MM01 within ERP. Therefore, it is essential that the material master is created prior to performing the mass extension of the product master.

I have created the following material in MM01 (in ERP), and there is no warehouse data maintained yet.We can check the list of products that do not have maintained warehouse data in the warehouse monitor belowMass upload/extend using -/SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT:

Refer the SAP KBA – “2602348 - Tools for Batch Warehouse Product Maintenance in S/4Hana embedded EWM”

In the KBA above, SAP explained how to use /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCT, and a sample file is also attached to the note. We can download the file named "scwm_mig_product_template_NEW.xlsx" and fill in our product master details there.

Below is the screenshot of the filled data from the file, and I converted this file to a .CSV format when saving it on the local drive.Execute /SCWM/MIG_PRODUCTAfter executing, we are getting the below error,The error occurs because we have not selected a field separator on the selection screen. If the field separator is not chosen, the system will not be able to read the spreadsheet data correctly. Therefore, ensure that the field separator is set to a comma (,) on the initial screen.

Now, let's fill in the fields and execute the process again.After executing the process again, we can now see that everything is green and there are no errors.We can check the extended product details in the warehouse monitor as below,Product Master extension using warehouse monitor:

Additionally, we can extend the product master using the warehouse monitor, and below we will test that functionality with the test case.

Please refer SAP KBA-“2627825 - Mass Creation of Warehouse Products from Monitor” & “2602348 - Tools for Batch Warehouse Product Maintenance in S/4Hana embedded EWM”In the screenshot below, we can see that the product is created in MM01, but the warehouse data is not extended.Follow the steps below to extend it,We have filled in the following details, which will apply to all the items selected on the previous screen. However, please note that this means all the selected products will have the same data entered here. If you need different data for specific items, those materials must be processed separately.The warehouse data for the materials has now been created.In the same way, we can maintain the storage type data and follow the screenshots for extending the storage type data view for the products.Enter the storage type where you will extend the product in the selection screen,Now, storage-type data is also maintained for the product.

Note:

We can use the warehouse monitor to make mass changes to warehouse products. If a product is already extended, the system will apply the changes based on the data provided. However, if the product is not extended in EWM, the monitor will create/extend the product according to the given data.

Ref:

2627825 - Mass Creation of Warehouse Products from Monitor

2602348 - Tools for Batch Warehouse Product Maintenance in S/4Hana embedded EWM

All data and screenshots in this documentation originate from the SAP S/4HANA 2023 demo system. This system is configured for training, testing, and demonstration purposes only and is not connected to any production environment. Any similarity to actual business data, entities, or individuals is purely coincidental.

Best Regards,

Kannan Chokkanathan

entry.title: The SAP Extended Warehouse Management FAQ Series: EWM clean core strategies and future roadmap
entry.content:
What are the different tiers for Clean Core development
The basis of Clean Core development for S/4HANA private Cloud customers is the S/4HANA extensibility guide and the 3-Tier model:

  • Tier-1: Cloud Extensibility Model. This model comprises custom development objects strictly following ABAP Cloud rules. Besides objects in their own software component, they can only access released objects. It can be either on Stack ABAP Cloud development, or side-by-side extensions on SAP BTP. 
  • Tier-2: Cloud API Enablement. This is the custom SAP interface developed in classic ABAP, that mitigates missing public SAP APIs. ABAP Cloud rules are enforced by the ATC (ABAP Test Cockpit) both for the development of wrapper objects, as well as the access to non-released SAP development objects.
  • Tier-3: Classic ABAP Extensions. We distinguish between new and old objects created here. For new objects in tier-3, ABAP Cloud rules shall be enforced by ATC. Existing objects in tier-3 may not follow the ABAP Cloud rules and can be exempted using a base line approach in ATC.


Picture source: SAP learning hub

Where are we in the concept of Clean Core for EWM in general?
For Tier-1 on-stack development, it is required to use only C1 released SAP APIs and extension points. For Tier-1 side-by-side Cloud development, C2 released SAP APIs are needed. Refer to the further linked document at the end of this blog for definition and differences between C1 and C2 release contracts.

For example, in EWM PCE, the following Odata V4 APIs are already available:

  • Warehouse Storage Bin – read
  • Warehouse Fixed Bin Assignment
  • Handling Unit
  • ..etc. Full list please refer to the SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Admittedly, there are not enough C1 released APIs in EWM PCE for on-stack custom extensions. We are aware of that, and we are working on it in our future roadmap. 

Alternatively, in cases where such a C1 API does not exist for Tier-1 on-stack development, customers/partners can still create their own wrapper APIs in Tier-2 based on classic ABAP APIs. These wrapper APIs can be released and used in Tier-1 on-stack developments. We have also talked with our customers regarding this Tier-2 approach, and we know there is project effort to enable such, as all standard DDIC objects used in the /SCWM/IF_API* interfaces will also need to be copied, etc.

In future, we will be working on more guidelines to provide our customers and partners with a list of classic stable EWM APIs in the cloudification repository, where content from other SAP application is already available. This will be published in the Business Accelerator Hub, and then can be used as reference for Tier-2 development in the future. This hopefully can be achieved later this year.

In general, Tier-3, classical custom development shall be used only in a controlled way to minimize the risk of potential upgrade issues. SAP note 2782080 provides a PDF document with several extensibility guides for specific areas of EWM. All these specific EWM extensibility guides offer upgrade safe extensibility methodologies, which have been proved in EWM on-premises since 10+ years and are also valid for S/4HANA private cloud edition. In addition, the above mentioned cloudification repository with EWM content that mentioned previously, will serve the same purpose to provide guidance for future Tier-3 custom development, as like the guidance for Tier-2 developments.

Again, our major goal for clean core concept from EWM PCE product perspective, is to ensure the future upgrade stability for our customers, by using stable standard APIs for on-stack developments.

What about custom development in RF framework?
EWM PCE customers can still implement classic ABAP in custom development in the RF framework, provided that the coding is according to the technical ABAP code guidelines. This would ensure upgradeability in the future. Refer to the previously mentioned SAP note for the detailed guidelines.
It is possible that when you run ATC checks, you can make sure that you use the check for “classic ABAP, ABAP Cloud rules not enforced”. This is because the variant “ABAP Cloud rules” in ATC checks, does not support SAPGUI screens, and SAPGUI technology framework is used in EWM RF framework.

Are there any other custom developments still allowed in EWM PCE?
This is a very good question. We do have some additional use cases where traditional custom developments are applied. One example is around the Warehouse Monitor, or our monitoring framework. We will not forbid 100% custom development in such areas, as long as there is sufficient business reason behind it, and the custom development is in accordance with the ABAP coding guidelines.

What about classic BADI implementation in EWM PCE?
In short, EWM PCE customers are still allowed to use classic BADI implementation, provided that the custom codes are in accordance with the ABAP code guidelines. Our main goal is to ensure upgrade stability and avoid potential issues. EWM is using this methodology since many years by providing the guidance of SAP note 2782080 and providing more than 700 BADIs as the upgrade safe “APIs”, for on-stack developments.

There are many new Cloud BADIs released in Public Cloud, why can we not reuse them in private Cloud?
This is because technically, the public cloud C1 released BADIs (in /SCWM/ES4_CORE_PTS) are different from the “Classic BADIs” (in /SCWM/ES_CORE_PTS) that we define in EWM PCE/OP. The set of new cloud BADIs has a new design in the BADI interfaces, the C1 released CDS views, and the remote APIs speak the same ‘language’ which is the new type system of GFNs(global field names).

What do we do in future in order to keep a close alignment of SAP’s Clean Core strategy as an EWM customer?
First of all, we would recommend our EWM customers to get a clearer picture on what we aim for clean core and make sure that we all understand the specialties in the EWM case. Of course, we encourage our customers to try out our available APIs from SAP Business Accelerator Hub and provide us with feedback on the potential gaps that are identified in your projects. We are keen to improve them, and we are working on it. Secondly, for custom developments, please check in the future (when available) the cloud repository for stable standard APIs and only use them for custom developments if possible. Finally, carefully think about the business necessity of having certain custom processes and keep in mind on the future efforts that it might introduce in the upgrades, such as regression tests, redesign efforts, and etc.  

FURTHER LINKS TO READ:
ABAP Language Versions, Release Contracts and Released APIs
Launching the RISE with SAP Methodology Dashboard for Your SAP Transformation Journey

entry.title: Automatic Invoice creation for EWM related Deliveries
entry.content:
Backend System – SAP S/4HANA 2020FP02

Overview  -

This blog offers comprehensive guidance on implementing automatic invoicing for scenarios related to Extended Warehouse Management (EWM).

Business Scenario

Initially, the business was using storage location-based stock management, incorporating automatic invoicing through a custom solution at the point of goods issue for the delivery document. However, with the implementation of Extended Warehouse Management (EWM), this enhancement extension failed to function as intended, hence, the real-time invoice creation process was interrupted. We were unable to identify an appropriate user exit to implement this enhancement. Consequently, the Goods Issue status of the delivery was not completed when the existing enhancement for the invoice creation program was executed, resulting in the failure of invoice creation.

The VF04 background schedule was proposed to the business. However, the business did not agree due to potential delays in invoice creation and the need to identify the invoice creator as the same user who performed the PGI. Using VF04 would result in the invoice creator being marked as the background user.

 

 

Step 1

In order to maintain output condition records based on the warehouse number, it is essential to include the LGNUM-ERP warehouse in the delivery output field catalog. Once this field is added, it will be available for creating a condition table and subsequently included in an access sequence. This enhancement ensures that the solution is restricted to Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) locations.

 

  1. Include field ZZLGNUM in communication structure KOMKBV2. This structure contains an Include that is provided for additional fields. Add the field with the name ZZLGNUM in Include KOMKBZ4.
  2. In structure KOMB in Include KOMBZ, you have to add the same field (same procedure as for item 1).
  3. In view V_T681F, you must create the same field with transaction SM31 under key B V2.
  4. 4. Program LVCOMU04 contains Perform USEREXIT_KOMKBV2_FILL. This Perform is in program RVCOMFZZ.
  5.  Modify the source code as follows: 

 

FORM_USEREXIT_KOMKBV2_FILL.

COM_KBV2-ZZLGNUM = COM_LIKP-LGNUM.

ENDFORM.

 

Step 2

 

Create an output condition table utilizing the Delivery Type and Warehouse Number (ZZLGNUM).

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 3

 

Create an access sequence using the previously created table to assign it to the condition type.

 

 

 

 

Step 4

Delivery Output Condition type creation.

 

 

Program – /SPE/DLV_IBGI_PROCESSING

Form Routine – ENTRY

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Step 5

 

Create a requirement to be used for the delivery output determination procedure.

Go to VOFM -> Requirements -> Output control

 

Create a new routine copying routine 1 code and add a wait for 2 seconds. This wait will make sure that the delivery is completed before the invoice is created. Otherwise, the invoice will be shown in the document flow before the PGI entry.

 

 

 

Maintain the Application as ‘V2’ and activate the routine.

 

Run the ‘RV80HGEN’ program using the SE38 transaction to activate the routine.

 

Note: This is client-specific activation.

 

 

Step 6

 

Create a new Output determination procedure with the newly created output type.

 

 

 

 

 

Assign the created requirement 901 to the procedure. This routine assignment ensures that invoice creation is delayed until after the PGI entry is created. It will make sure that the output type is assigned to the delivery only after the PGI is completed, preventing invoice generation from happening before the PGI entry.

 

 

Step 7

Assign the delivery output procedure to the Delivery type.

 

 

 

 

 

Step 8

 

Output condition maintain

Go to VV21 in order to maintain the output condition for the ZREA output type.

 

 

 

 

 

 

Output type ZREA will be available after the Goods Issue has been posted. Consequently, the invoice related to the post-goods issue will be created automatically.

 

Conclusion –

This configuration and the developments will ensure that invoice creation will be automated without major customization for the SAP standard solution.

 

Special thanks to the Altria Consulting team @Kalana_Chathuranga_Herath, @HasiniTW, @Nilupul_Rambukwelle, @Dushan_Sasmitha and @Ravindu_Herath.
